A canonical example of this form of analysis is provided by the normal model with unknown mean and variance.

paragraph_content.content: SUMMARY OF ELEMENTARY MODELING AND COMPUTATION

page_header_content.content: Summary of Elementary Modeling and Computation

paragraph_content.content: The lack of multi-parameter models permitting easy calculation of posterior distributions is NOT a major practical handicap for three main reasons.

item_content.content: 1. When there are few parameters, posterior inference in non-conjugate multi-parameter models can be obtained by simple simulation methods.
2. Sophisticated models can often be represented in a hierarchical or conditional manner, for which effective computational strategies are available.

page_header_content.content: Summary of Elementary Modeling and Computation

paragraph_content.content: 3. We can often apply a normal approximation to the posterior distribution, and therefore the conjugate structure of the normal model can play an important role in practice, well beyond its application to explicitly normal sampling models.

page_header_content.content: Summary of Elementary Modeling and Computation

paragraph_content.content: The following summarizes what we have done so far and foreshadows the general methods.

item_content.content: 1. Write the likelihood part of the model,
, ignoring any factors that are free of
- If prior information is well-formulated, include it in
- Otherwise use a weakly informative prior distribution or temporarily set
constant, with the understanding that the prior density can be altered later to include additional information or structure.

page_header_content.content: Summary of Elementary Modeling and Computation

item_content.content: 3. Create a crude estimate of the parameters,
, for use as a starting point and a comparison to the computation in the next step.
, from the posterior distribution. Use the sample draws to compute the posterior density of any functions of
from the sampling distribution conditional on the drawn value

page_header_content.content: Summary of Elementary Modeling and Computation

paragraph_content.content: For non-conjugate models, step 4 above can be difficult.
Various methods have been developed to draw posterior simulations in complicated models.
Occasionally, high-dimensional problems can be solved by combining analytical and numerical simulation methods.
has only one or two components, it is possible to draw simulations by computing on a grid.
